message: Run created: setup doc readme git, codegraph, . alligne with software industry standard
Task started: Create standardized README.md with project overview and setup instructions

details.text: A fully client-side, offline-first personal IELTS study system. Track vocabulary, reading, listening, writing, speaking, grammar, and mock tests — all in your browser with no backend required.
- **Vocabulary Management** — Add, tag, filter, and review words with spaced repetition (SM-2)
- **Daily Plan** — Plan and track daily study tasks across all IELTS skills
- **Skill Tracking** — Log reading, listening, writing, and speaking practice sessions
- **Progress Analytics** — Charts and stats for study days, hours, skill balance, and band trends
- **Import/Export** — JSON import/export for backup and migration
- **Mock Test Tracker** — Log mock test scores and track band progress over time
- **AI Assistant** — Optional OpenAI-compatible API integration for feedback and suggestions
- **Global Search** — Full-text search across all your data
- **Offline-First PWA** — Works offline after initial load; data stored in IndexedDB
- **Grammar Notes** — Organize grammar topics with examples and common mistakes
- **Mistake Notebook** — Record and review mistakes with correction tracking

README.md created at `/Users/phamthanhhung/Desktop/MyProject/IELTS/README.md` with all standard sections: Project Title, Description (features + tech stack), Installation, Usage, Development (project structure + scripts + data architecture), Testing, Linting & Type Checking, Contributing, and License. Formatted with proper Markdown headings, lists, tables, and code blocks.

details.text: When testing, code that causes React state updates should be wrapped into act(...):

An update to VocabularyReview inside a test was not wrapped in act(...).
When testing, code that causes React state updates should be wrapped into act(...):
This ensures that you're testing the behavior the user would see in the browser. Learn more at https://react.dev/link/wrap-tests-with-act
